rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR TEMPORARY PROTECTIVE ORDER, PERMANENT PROTECTIVE ORDER
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant E .I. Dupont de Nemours and Company¿s motion for protective order is denied. The discovery sought by plaintiffs meets the standard of discovery relevance and defendant has not shown that responding to the discovery would impose an undue burden on it. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS RUBINO et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Depositions Of Persons Most Knowledgeable Custodians Of Records Of Cyprus Amax Minerals Company; R.T. Vanderbilt Company; And E.I. Dupont De Nemours Company, And Production Of Documents And Things
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel depositions of person most knowledgeable and custodian of records of defendant Cyprus Amax Minerals is off calendar at the request of the moving parties. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel depositions of person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and production of documents from defendant R. T. Vanderbilt Company is granted. The discovery sought by plaintiffs meets the standard of discovery relevance and defendant has not shown that responding to the discovery would impose an undue burden on it. Plaintiffs¿ motion to compel depositions of person most knowledgeable and custodian of records and production of documents from Defendant E. I. Dupont de Nemours and Company is granted. The discovery sought by plaintiffs meets the standard of discovery relevance and defendant has not shown that responding to the discovery would impose an undue burden on it. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ORMAN SMITH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Linde, Inc. To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Linde Inc.¿s motion to compel further responses to requests for admission is granted. The requests are relevant on Proposition 51 allocation and the number of requests is justified by the complexity inherent in multi-party asbestos cases. Plaintiff must admit or deny whether asbestos fibers attributable to the entities named in the requests were a substantial factor in causing plaintiff¿s alleged asbestos-related disease or state that he lacks sufficient information to admit or deny. If the entity is a bankruptcy trust, the request shall be construed as asking for information about the bankrupt entity, not the trust. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ALAN RAY VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Admission And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Linde Inc.¿s motion to compel further responses to requests for admission is granted. The requests are relevant on Proposition 51 allocation and the number of requests is justified by the complexity inherent in multi-party asbestos cases. Plaintiff must admit or deny whether asbestos fibers attributable to the entities named in the requests were a substantial factor in causing plaintiff¿s alleged asbestos-related disease or state that he lacks sufficient information to admit or deny. If the entity is a bankruptcy trust, the request shall be construed as asking for information about the bankrupt entity, not the trust. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Shirley Batts To Perpetuate Testimony Of Jim Batts, Jr By Videotaped Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Petitioner Shirley Batts¿ petition to perpetuate testimony of Jim Batts, Jr. is granted. C.C.P. § 2035.010 allows a person who expects to be a party to an action to obtain discovery for the purpose of perpetuating the testimony of another person. Petitioner¿s request to have Mr. Batts¿ deposition held at or near his home in Russellville, Arkansas is granted. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Amended Petition Of Norman Juelch To Perpetuate Testimony Of Joyce Juelch By Videotaped Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 5. Petitioner Norman Juelch¿s petition to perpetuate testimony of Joyce Juelch is granted. C.C.P. § 2035.010 allows a person who expects to be a party to an action to obtain discovery for the purpose of perpetuating the testimony of another person. Petitioner¿s request to have Mrs. Juelch¿s deposition held at or near her home in Cottonwood, California is granted. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Re: Petition Of Betty Palmer To Perpetuate Testimony Of Richard Palmer By Videotaped Deposition
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Tuesday, July 13, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 6. PETITIONER BETTY PALMER's Petition Of Betty Palmer To Perpetuate Testimony Of Richard Palmer By Videotaped Deposition is granted. No Opposition filed. =(220/HEK)
